prose: Fukui reactivity indices for furan (C₄H₄O, 9 atoms) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS with density fitting), gas phase, charge 0/multiplicity 1 for the neutral, charge +1/mult 2 for the cation, charge −1/mult 2 for the anion. Charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme (PySCF). The geometry was taken as-supplied from the input .xyz file (not re-optimized at this level). Integrity checks all passed (trustworthy=true); sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f−)=1.0000. No engine warnings were raised. The headline value reported is the maximum f+ (electrophilic Fukui index), found at atom 5 (C), f+ = 1.9242931. The α-carbons (atoms 4 and 5, flanking the oxygen) show the largest f+ and f− values, consistent with furan's known α-site reactivity. Negative f+ values on O and H atoms are a known Mulliken finite-difference artifact with delocalized charge. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /path/to/furan.xyz. Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260702-200923_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
